methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Thunder Tiger operates in the Defense & Security sector as a publicly traded company, having raised $22M+ as of 2025 under founder and current CEO Gene Su. The valuation landscape comprises a broader universe of 21 same-sector peers, including 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies. Notable private peers include Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

A peer-median valuation approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient clustering within narrow stage and valuation-tier filters. While the Defense & Security sector contains 21 total peers, cross-referencing for same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-tier companies yielded fewer than three datapoints—below the threshold required for robust median analysis. Relying on all-sector medians would introduce substantial measurement error given Thunder Tiger's specialized positioning.

The analysis is constrained by limited comparability on standardized financial and operational metrics across the public and private peer set. Without sufficient same-stage clustering, triangulation across revenue multiples, growth rates, or profitability benchmarks cannot be meaningfully executed. Establishing a defensible valuation range therefore requires either (1) collection of deeper operational data from the identified peer universe to establish scaled comparisons, or (2) application of discounted cash flow or comparable transaction methods using company-specific projections. Current data density does not support a quantified valuation conclusion.
